assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: Response mentions update frequency differences but doesn't explain SLA differences between channels or distinguish between minor vs patch version handling

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states that changing release channel 'triggers automatic upgrade to Stable version' when channel changes don't actually trigger immediate version changes

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: Response doesn't address the specific concern about patch availability when current version isn't yet on the target channel

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Correctly provides 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --location=YOUR_LOCATION --release-channel=stable' command

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: Includes the gcloud container get-server-config command to check available versions on Stable channel

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: Explains that Stable has different update frequency (every 2-3 months vs 2-4 weeks) and more predictable maintenance windows

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the Extended channel as an alternative option

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions planning for maintenance window and that upgrades can take 30+ minutes
